methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $73M - $3.6B.

data.narrative:
T1 Energy (formerly FREYR Battery) is a publicly traded climate and energy company founded by Tom Einar Jensen. The company has raised over $1 billion to date. The comparable universe consists of four same-sector, same-stage peers: QuantumScape ($3.6B), Enovix ($1.0B), Solid Power ($504M), and LanzaTech ($73M).

The peer-median method is applicable and forms the basis of this analysis. The median valuation across these four public Climate & Energy companies is $752M, with a range spanning $73M to $3.6B. This wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity in technology maturity, commercialization progress, and market sentiment within the frontier energy sector. The peer-median approach captures the central tendency of comparable stage and sector positioning without bias toward the outliers.

The triangulated valuation range is $639M to $865M, centered on the $752M median. Given that only one valuation method is currently applicable, this range should be treated as indicative rather than robust. Key uncertainties include the specific technology differentiation of T1 Energy relative to the peer set, relative progress toward commercialization, capital intensity of scaling, and near-term financing needs. Investors should supplement this analysis with operational due diligence on unit economics, technical risk, and go-to-market strategy to refine the valuation range.
